notes: URLs marked verified_accessible:true appeared directly in search engine results with PDF content confirming accessibility. URLs marked false are page-based redirects, .docx files, or returned 403 on automated fetch (though may be accessible via browser). FERC.gov direct PDF links return 403 to automated requests but are authoritative public government documents widely used in practice.

documents.notes: FERC standard pro forma SGIA template. All executed SGIAs filed with FERC must substantially conform to this template. Returns 403 to automated fetch but is a well-known public government document found in search results. Governing instrument for generators <=20 MW.

FERC standard pro forma LGIA template. Governs generators >20 MW. Transmission providers must file tariff conforming to this template per FERC Order 2003. Returns 403 to automated requests but publicly available.

documents.doc_type: Large Generator Interconnection Agreement (Alternate Pro Forma)
documents.notes: Alternate FERC pro forma LGIA document. Used for executed bilateral agreements. Found in FERC search results alongside LGIA.pdf.

documents.doc_type: Small Generator Interconnection Agreement (Pro Forma - Alternate)
documents.notes: Alternate FERC pro forma SGIA document title includes 'SMALL GENERATOR INTERCONNECTION AGREEMENT (SGIA)'. Companion to SGIA.pdf.

documents.doc_type: Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (Pro Forma SGIP)
documents.notes: FERC pro forma Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP). Describes the process for interconnecting generators <=20 MW including pre-application reports, Fast Track, and Study Track processes.

documents.doc_type: Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (Pro Forma LGIP)
documents.notes: FERC pro forma Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP). Covers Feasibility Study, System Impact Study, and Facilities Study processes for generators >20 MW.

Sample interconnection agreement for the U.S. Army Fort Carson 2 MW ground-mount PV project funded through a PPA. Available via DOE FEMP downloads page; requires browser to access PDF download. Docket: FEMP Federal PPA Sample Documents.

PJM FERC filing (Docket Nos. ER24-994-000, ER24-995-000, ER24-1001-000) related to Welcome Solar ISA cancellation. References originally executed ISAs from ER22-1908 and ER22-2112. Shows real-world ISA milestones including Site Permits, Major Equipment, and Substantial Site Work.

2024 Brattle Group Generator Interconnection Scorecard ranking CAISO, PJM, MISO, and other ISO/RTOs on interconnection outcomes. Includes analysis of agreement timelines, cost allocation, and study processes. Useful reference for understanding interconnection practices.

ACP Interconnection 101 fact sheet (June 2023). Explains the FERC interconnection process, queue statistics (259 GW in PJM at end of 2021, dominated by solar at 116 GW), and key reform context. Educational reference.

LBNL 2023 analysis of interconnection costs in the PJM territory. Covers 86% of new unique generators requesting interconnection in PJM from 2000 to 2022. Notes data accessibility challenges for prospective developers. Useful for cost estimation in project finance models.

documents.doc_type: PURPA Power Purchase Agreement (Schedule 37 New QF)
documents.notes: Executed PURPA QF PPA between OR Solar 6, LLC and PacifiCorp (dba Pacific Power & Light). Oregon Schedule 37 Power Purchase Agreement for new QF <= 10,000 kW. Filed under Oregon PUC Docket RE 142. References FERC regulations 18 CFR Part 292.

documents.doc_type: PURPA Power Purchase Agreement (Schedule 37 New QF)
documents.notes: Executed PURPA QF PPA between EW Auna Solar 2, LLC and PacifiCorp. Photovoltaic facility in Klamath Falls, Klamath County, Oregon. Facility Capacity Rating: 2,900 kW. Oregon Schedule 37 New QF agreement, effective August 20, 2014. Docket RE 142.

documents.doc_type: PURPA Power Purchase Agreement (Schedule 37 New QF)
documents.notes: Executed PURPA QF PPA between Norwest Energy 9, LLC and PacifiCorp. Photovoltaic facility in Pendleton, Umatilla County, Oregon. Facility Capacity Rating: 6,000 kW. Oregon Schedule 37 New QF, effective August 20, 2014. Docket RE 142.

documents.doc_type: PURPA Power Purchase Agreement (Schedule 37 Off-System QF)
documents.notes: PURPA PPA between Three Sisters Irrigation District and PacifiCorp. Oregon Standard Power Purchase Agreement (Off-system QF) format. Includes Section 20 PURPA repeal clause. Docket RE 142.

Oregon PUC proceeding document from RE 142 docket involving Lacomb Irrigation District PURPA QF PPA. Oregon Standard Power Purchase Agreement (Existing QF), effective August 11, 2016. Discusses PURPA filing obligations.

documents.doc_type: PURPA QF Standard Contract Staff Report
documents.notes: Oregon PUC Staff Report for AR 631 PURPA rulemaking on QF Standard Contracts. Covers PacifiCorp (Pacific Power), Portland General Electric, and Idaho Power contract standardization. Rulemaking hearing January 12, 2023. Useful for understanding Oregon PURPA contracting framework.

documents.doc_type: Solar PPA and Contract Suite Overview (Template)
documents.notes: IRENA and Terrawatt Initiative Open Solar Contracts 2019 review document. Describes suite of six standardized templates for small/medium grid-connected solar PV: PPA, Implementation Agreement, O&M Agreement, Supply Agreement, Installation Agreement, Finance Facility Term Sheet. Free and open source.

DOE FEMP landing page with links to sample PPA documents from completed federal projects: Camp Roberts (Army), Princeton Plasma Physics Lab, GSA Sacramento, NREL, Fort Carson, NASA. Navigate to individual documents from this page. Not a direct PDF but aggregates multiple downloadable documents.

Gemini Solar Project Final RMPA and EIS Volume I. ~690 MWac PV project on ~7,100 acres of BLM land, ~30 miles northeast of Las Vegas, Clark County, Nevada. Developed by Solar Partners XI, LLC. One of the most detailed BLM solar EIS documents available. NEPA project #100498.

Signed Record of Decision for Gemini Solar Project, dated May 8, 2020. Authorizes BLM right-of-way grant for construction, operation, maintenance, and decommissioning of the ~690 MW PV facility. Includes all appendices. NEPA project #100498.

Yellow Pine Solar Project Final EIS Volume I, dated August 28, 2020. Up to 500 MW PV with battery storage on ~3,000 acres BLM land, ~10 miles southeast of Pahrump, Clark County, Nevada. Includes GridLiance West 230-kV substation and transmission line. NEPA project #81665.

Signed ROD for Yellow Pine Solar Project, November 6, 2020. Authorizes 30-year ROW grants for Yellow Pine Solar, LLC and GridLiance West, LLC. ~3,100 acres disturbed. 500 MW PV + storage + 230-kV transmission. NEPA project #81665.

Environmental Assessment for Playa Solar Project, one of three First Solar projects in the Dry Lake Solar Energy Zone (SEZ), Clark County, Nevada. Part of ~3,083 acres/440 MW combined BLM competitive lease auction (June 30, 2014). Completed in under 10 months. NEPA project #42099.

documents.doc_type: Environmental Assessment (EA) - Gen-Tie and Access Roads
documents.notes: Final EA for San Juan Solar Gen-Tie, Collector Power Lines, and Access Roads. Connects a solar+storage facility on private land to PNM substation, ~3 miles north of Waterflow, NM. 345-kV gen-tie with ~47 pole structures. Published April 28, 2021 on BLM NM ePlanning. Final EA dated January 5, 2022.

Draft EIS for Bonanza Solar Project, located ~5 miles west of Indian Springs, ~30 miles northwest of Las Vegas, Clark County, Nevada. NOI published June 5, 2023. BLM ePlanning project #2020905. Recent utility-scale solar project in Nevada.

2023 Draft Solar Programmatic EIS Volume 1 (508-compliant version, released January 10, 2024). Proposes updated BLM Western Solar Plan covering 11 western states. Would make 31+ million acres available for potential solar development. Includes proposed RMP amendments. National-level policy document.

Scoping Report for Esmeralda 7 Solar Project, BLM ePlanning project #2020804. Covers topics including Social Values, Economic Conditions, Environmental Justice, Soils, Visual Resources, Transportation and Public Safety, and future NEPA steps. Early-stage NEPA document.

Draft Biological Assessment for Gemini Solar Project, dated June 14, 2019. Covers threatened and endangered species consultation for the 690 MW project. Companion document to the FEIS. NEPA project #100498. Relevant for endangered species act compliance in solar project finance.

documents.doc_type: MISO Definitive Planning Phase (DPP) System Impact Study - Phase I
documents.notes: MISO DPP 2022 Phase I Study Report for Mantle Rock Solar LLC. Filed as exhibit in Kentucky PSC case 2024-00050. DPP 2022 analyzed 778 interconnection requests totaling 141.4 GW nameplate. Screening analysis made available March 12, 2023. Uses Pearl Street Technologies SUGAR software. Appendix B (network upgrades) contains CEII.

MISO Feasibility Study Report for Sebree Solar II, LLC. Filed as Exhibit 9, Attachment A in Kentucky PSC Case 2022-00131. Draft received 9/21/2022, finalized 10/4/2022; subsequent draft 1/31/2023, finalized 2/14/2023. Shows typical MISO interconnection study timeline and output format.

documents.doc_type: Loss of Load Expectation (LOLE) Resource Adequacy Study
documents.notes: MISO Planning Year 2025-2026 LOLE Study Report. Resource adequacy study determining whether the MISO system meets 0.1 days/year loss of load expectation standard. Includes solar and storage capacity contributions. Used in capacity market planning and relevant for assessing solar project's contribution to resource adequacy in MISO projects.

documents.doc_type: Wind and Solar Capacity Credit Study Report
documents.notes: MISO 2022 Wind and Solar Capacity Credit Report. As of December 2022: 4,323 MW nameplate front-of-meter solar in MISO (+85% YoY). New solar resources receive class average 50% capacity credit in first year. Used in production estimates and resource adequacy planning.

documents.doc_type: Wind and Solar Capacity Credit Study Report
documents.notes: MISO 2023 Wind and Solar Capacity Credit Report. Updated solar capacity credit methodology and accreditation data. Important for solar project finance modeling (capacity value = revenue in capacity markets).

documents.source: City of Laramie, WY (Albany County Public Records)
documents.notes: Publicly filed solar lease and option agreement for the Sailor Solar Energy Center in Albany County, Wyoming. From City of Laramie agenda records. Includes option period, recording requirements, and SNDA provisions. Typical municipal solar land lease structure.

documents.doc_type: Land Lease Option and Lease Agreement (Solar Farm)
documents.notes: Public municipal solar land lease template from City of Laconia, NH. Includes option period structure, memorandum of lease recording rights, and tax incentive preservation clause ('amend if either party becomes ineligible for tax credit'). Document number 15123468.2.

documents.doc_type: Solar Land Lease FAQ / Guidance Document
documents.notes: Pennsylvania PUC guidance document on solar land lease agreements for landowners (December 2022). Explains commercial solar lease structure, option periods, per-acre payment ranges, removal/restoration obligations, and landowner protections. Useful for understanding typical solar land lease terms.

NYSERDA NY-Sun program solar land lease agreement template in Word (.docx) format. Official state agency template for New York solar projects. Publicly available for download. Note: Word document not PDF.

documents.doc_type: Cultural Resources Management Plan (BLM ROW Document)
documents.notes: Cultural Resources Management Plan for the Rough Hat Clark Solar Project. 400 MW AC solar PV + 700 MW energy storage on ~2,433 acres BLM land in Pahrump Valley, Clark County, Nevada. Applicant seeking 30-year BLM ROW grant. Shows typical site-control document package for BLM projects. BLM project #2019992.

documents.doc_type: IRS Notice - Energy Community Bonus Credit
documents.notes: IRS Notice 2023-29: Energy Community Bonus Credit Amounts or Rates under IRA Sections 45, 45Y, 48, and 48E. Defines energy communities (brownfield sites, coal closure areas, statistical areas with fossil fuel employment), nameplate capacity location test (50% threshold), and provides Appendices 1-3 with qualifying area lists.

documents.doc_type: IRS Notice - Energy Community Bonus Credit (Modification)
documents.notes: IRS Notice 2024-30: Modifies Notice 2023-29 by expanding the Nameplate Capacity Attribution Rule and adding NAICS industry codes to the Fossil Fuel Employment rate table. Key IRA tax credit reference for solar project siting near energy communities.

documents.doc_type: IRS Publication - Pre-Filing Registration User Guide
documents.notes: IRS Publication 5884: Pre-Filing Registration user guide for elective pay and transferability under IRA Sections 6417 and 6418. Required step before transferring clean energy tax credits. Each eligible credit property must have its own registration number; registration valid for one taxable year.

Treasury/IRS Final Regulations under IRC Section 6418 (IRA transferability), published April 30, 2024. 89 Fed. Reg. 34770. Effective July 1, 2024. Covers transfer elections, 'paid in cash' requirement, partial transfers, pre-filing registration, passive activity rules, partnership elections, and 20% excessive transfer penalty. PDF version available via Federal Register sidebar.

Real-world Tax Credit Transfer Agreement filed with SEC EDGAR. Effective December 21, 2023. Includes transfer election under Section 6418(a) of the IRC. Shows actual TCTA deal structure: transferor, transferee, credit amount, cash consideration, registration requirements. Good template for understanding bespoke TCTA terms pre-standardization.

documents.doc_type: Annual Report / 10-K (YieldCo with Solar Tax Equity Disclosures)
documents.source: Clearway Energy, Inc. (SEC Filing via Investor Relations)
documents.notes: Clearway Energy, Inc. 2023 Annual Report. Clearway owns 7,000+ MW of wind, solar, and natural gas assets. Report includes detailed disclosures on tax equity partnership flip structures, ITC/PTC utilization, and IRA transferability impacts. Also available on SEC EDGAR (CIK 1567683). May require browser-based download from investor relations site.

LBNL Queued Up 2024 Edition: Characteristics of Power Plants Seeking Transmission Interconnection (data through end of 2023). Key stats: 1,570+ GW generation + 1,030 GW storage in active queues; solar = 1,086 GW largest share; median queue-to-COD time >4 years for 2018-2023 builds. Essential reference for solar project finance market context.

LBNL Queued Up 2025 Edition: data through end of 2024. Active solar queue: 956 GW (-12% YoY); storage: 890 GW (-13%); gas: 136 GW (+72%). Median queue-to-COD time doubled from <2 years (2000-2007) to >4 years (2018-2024). New analysis on FERC Order 2023 reform implementation. Published December 15, 2025.

NREL PVWatts Version 5 Manual (Dobos, 2014). NREL/TP-6A20-62641. Describes all sub-models, algorithms, and hidden parameters used by the PVWatts calculator. PVWatts V5 predicts on average 8% more annual energy than V1. Standard methodology reference for IE production estimates. V8 API is current production version.

NREL SAM Photovoltaic Model Technical Reference Update (2018). NREL/TP-6A20-67399. Documents the basic algorithm of SAM's PV performance model: up to four subarrays with independent tracking, surface angles, shading/soiling, and DC losses parameters. Reference for System Advisor Model (SAM) methodology used in IE reports.

NREL System Advisor Model (SAM) General Description (Version 2017.9.5). NREL/TP-6A20-70414. SAM is the industry-standard techno-economic feasibility model for renewable energy projects used by developers, policy makers, and financial planners. Open source; free download at sam.nrel.gov.

documents.source: Connecticut Green Bank / Green Earth Energy
documents.notes: EPC Agreement template (Exhibit D-2) from Connecticut Green Bank financing package for Green Earth Energy. Template 1: Engineering, Procurement & Construction Agreement. Shows typical commercial solar EPC contract structure including scope of work, performance guarantees, liquidated damages, and payment schedule.

PwC analysis: Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) contracts in the solar sector. Covers key EPC contract provisions, risk allocation between developers and contractors, performance guarantees, liquidated damages, and bankability considerations. Useful reference for understanding standard EPC terms in utility-scale solar project finance.

documents.source: DOE Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE)
documents.notes: DOE EERE guide to procuring solar for federal facilities including EPC contract considerations, design-bid-build vs. design-build approaches, SAM.gov procurement process, and ESPC/PPA options. Links to FEMP procurement specifications templates for on-site solar PV. Page URL; navigate to specific templates.

IRENA Open Solar Contracts O&M Agreement Review Draft. Key document in any solar project finance transaction alongside the EPC contract. Covers scope of services, reimbursable costs, spares requirements, performance metrics, annual reports, record-keeping, remote monitoring, fees, metering, and indemnification. Free and open source.

documents.source: County of Amador, CA / SitelogIQ (Public Records via Legistar)
documents.notes: Executed O&M Agreement between County of Amador and SitelogIQ for county solar installation, dated June 16, 2020. Real-world executed document from public records. Shows typical commercial O&M agreement structure for government-owned solar systems.

DOE FEMP O&M RFP Template for Government-Owned Solar PV Systems. Customizable template for federal agencies procuring O&M services. Free public resource. Navigate from this page to download the template document. Part of FEMP's solar procurement resource library.

FERC Order No. 2023: Improvements to Generator Interconnection Procedures and Agreements. FR Doc. 2023-16628, published September 6, 2023, effective November 6, 2023. Docket RM22-14-000. Mandates cluster study process, study delay penalties ($1,000-$2,500/day), IBR modeling standards, and financial readiness requirements. PDF available via Federal Register sidebar.

documents.source: NextEra Energy / NextEra Energy Partners (Investor Relations)
documents.notes: NextEra Energy 2023 Annual Report. NextEra Energy Partners owns ~10,118 MW contracted wind, solar, and storage assets. Includes detailed disclosures on: IRA impact on tax credits, transferability provisions, partnership flip structures, long-term PPA contracts, and renewable project finance. Industry benchmark for utility-scale solar company financials.

documents.doc_type: NREL Technical Reference - SAM and PVWatts Update
documents.notes: NREL 2023 presentation/report on System Advisor Model (SAM) and PVWatts. NREL/TP-7A40-87939. Presented at DOE EECBG Webinar Series, November 3, 2023. Provides current SAM/PVWatts overview including V8 updates (bifacial modules, 2020 TMY data). Relevant for understanding production modeling methodology.

MISO Series 1A Futures Report (published November 1, 2023). Long-range transmission planning study analyzing future capacity scenarios for the MISO footprint. Includes solar and storage integration scenarios, transmission needs assessment, and resource adequacy implications. Key reference for understanding transmission planning context for solar project finance.

Oregon PUC AHD Report for AR 631 PURPA Rulemaking on QF Standard Contracts. Covers history of attempt to create standardized PPA contracts for PacifiCorp, Portland General Electric, and Idaho Power. NOI November 23, 2022; rulemaking hearing January 12, 2023. Explains why standardization failed and resulting framework.

documents.source: DOE Office of Electricity / i2X Initiative
documents.notes: DOE i2X (Interconnection Innovation Exchange) DER Interconnection Roadmap (January 2025). Outlines pathway for improving distributed energy resource (DER) interconnection including solar+storage. Covers queue management, standardization, digitization, and stakeholder coordination. Companion to FERC Order 2023 for distribution-level interconnection.
